---
title: "Online Books"
---

My summaries on what I have learned on econometrics and causal inference.

### [Econometrics Guide](https://xiangao.github.io/econometrics-guide/)

A practical guide to applied econometrics with examples in R and Stata.

### [Topics on econometrics and causal inference](https://xiangao.github.io/blog_book/)

Collected notes and posts on applied econometrics and causal inference, compiled into book format.

### [Causal Econometrics Guide](https://xiangao.github.io/causal_econometrics_guide/)

A guide to modern causal inference methods for applied researchers, with R implementations. Covers identification and potential outcomes (ATE, ATT, LATE, CATE, and QTE on a single DGP), DAG-based identification and estimation, nonparametric methods (TMLE, DoubleML), difference-in-differences, IV and regression discontinuity, shift-share (Bartik) IV, Poisson IV, causal mediation, causal discovery (PC and GES for observed variables, FCI and RFCI for latent variables), and a graph-to-estimate pipeline using `causaleffect` and `dagitty`.

### [Causal Econometrics in Julia](https://xiangao.github.io/causal_econometrics_julia/)

A hands-on guide to causal inference and econometrics using Julia, covering identification, potential outcomes, five estimands on one DGP, estimation (RA, IPW, AIPW, TMLE, DoubleML), distributional treatment effects, difference-in-differences (ETWFE, synthetic control, synthetic DiD, TASC), shift-share (Bartik) IV with Rotemberg weights and BHJ shock-level inference, IV and regression discontinuity, Poisson IV, mediation analysis, and causal discovery (PC, RSL-D, FCI, L-MARVEL) with a graph-to-estimate pipeline via `CausalGraphs.jl`.
